Now, time and memory: We have up to 2n nodes, each component stores a vector of cities it belongs to. The total number of entries across all components is at most the sum of degrees of cities, i.e., at most 2n, but due to overlapping copies we may have duplicates? In our representation, each city appears in a component's cities list if it has that component. Since each city can be in at most 2 components, total sum of sizes across all component city lists is ≤ 2n. That's good. Also each component's sec map entries correspond to each city with two components, and each such city contributes 1 entry to each of the two components' sec maps (key being the other component). So total sec entries across all components ≤ 2n. So memory is O(n). Good.

Now, we need to ensure that when we merge, we don't cause the large component's city list to become huge and we iterate over it many times. The small-to-large ensures we iterate over smaller ones each time.

Now, implementation details in C++:

- We'll define struct Comp as:

struct Comp {
    vector<int> cities;
    int deg1;
    unordered_map<int, int> sec;
};

We'll have vector<Comp> comp.

- We'll need to keep track of component size for DSU union condition; we can just use comp[ru].cities.size().

- In unionComp, we'll need to use comp[ru] and comp[rv] which are references; after we possibly swap ru and rv, we should update our notion of small and large.

- To avoid copying large structures when swapping, we can just swap the indices ru and rv and then treat ru as small, rv as large.

Implementation of unionComp:

void unionComp(int ru, int rv) {
    if (comp[ru].cities.size() > comp[rv].cities.size()) {
        swap(ru, rv);
    }
    // now ru is smaller
    Comp& small = comp[ru];
    Comp& large = comp[rv];
    int64_t delta = 0;
    int overlap = 0;
    if (small.sec.count(rv)) overlap = small.sec[rv];
    int large_excl_total = large.cities.size() - overlap;

    for (int city : small.cities) {
        bool has_rv = false;
        int D = -1;
        for (int cid : city_comps[city]) {
            if (cid == rv) {
                has_rv = true;
            } else if (cid != ru) {
                D = cid;  // the other component, could be -1 if not found (should be exactly one other if any)
            }
        }
        if (has_rv) {
            continue; // overlapping, no new connections
        }
        // city exclusive to small
        int already = 0;
        if (D != -1) {
            auto it = large.sec.find(D);
            if (it != large.sec.end()) already = it->second;
        }
        delta += large_excl_total - already;
    }

    ans += delta;

    // Now merge small into large
    for (int city : small.cities) {
        bool has_rv = false;
        int X = -1;
        for (int cid : city_comps[city]) {
            if (cid == rv) {
                has_rv = true;
            } else if (cid != ru) {
                X = cid;
            }
        }
        if (has_rv) {
            // overlapping: city in both ru and rv
            // update large: decrement sec[ru], increase deg1
            large.sec[ru]--;
            if (large.sec[ru] == 0) large.sec.erase(ru);
            large.deg1++;
            // city_comps: remove ru
            vector<int>& clist = city_comps[city];
            clist.erase(remove(clist.begin(), clist.end(), ru), clist.end()); // easier: since size small, simple loop
            // Actually we can just do:
            for (auto it = clist.begin(); it != clist.end(); ++it) {
                if (*it == ru) {
                    clist.erase(it);
                    break;
                }
            }
            // rv remains in clist (still present)
        } else {
            if (X == -1) {
                // city only in ru
                // add to large
                large.cities.push_back(city);
                large.deg1++;
                // update city_comps: replace ru with rv
                vector<int>& clist = city_comps[city];
                // remove ru, add rv
                for (auto it = clist.begin(); it != clist.end(); ++it) {
                    if (*it == ru) {
                        clist.erase(it);
                        break;
                    }
                }
                clist.push_back(rv);
            } else {
                // city has ru and X
                large.cities.push_back(city);
                large.sec[X]++;
                // update component X
                Comp& compX = comp[X];
                compX.sec[ru]--;
                if (compX.sec[ru] == 0) compX.sec.erase(ru);
                compX.sec[rv]++;
                // update city_comps: replace ru with rv
                vector<int>& clist = city_comps[city];
                for (auto it = clist.begin(); it != clist.end(); ++it) {
                    if (*it == ru) {
                        *it = rv;  // replace directly, then break
                        break;
                    }
                }
                // Ensure rv not dup? Since clist had ru and X, replacing ru with rv could produce duplicate if X == rv? But X != rv because has_rv false, so safe.
            }
        }
    }
    // Set DSU parent
    parent[ru] = rv;
    // Clear small to free memory (optional)
    small.cities.clear();
    small.sec.clear();
    small.deg1 = 0;
}

Let's implement linkCityToComponent:

void linkCityToComponent(int city, int comp_id) {
    Comp& cdata = comp[comp_id];
    vector<int>& clist = city_comps[city];
    if (clist.empty()) {
        // city new to any component
        clist.push_back(comp_id);
        cdata.cities.push_back(city);
        cdata.deg1++;
    } else {
        // city already has one component (since max 1 before)
        int other = clist[0]; // only one
        // update other component
        Comp& otherComp = comp[other];
        // city was deg1 in other, now becomes sec
        otherComp.deg1--;
        otherComp.sec[comp_id]++;
        // update new component
        cdata.cities.push_back(city);
        cdata.sec[other]++; // because city now has comp_id and other
        // update city
        clist.push_back(comp_id);
        // now city has two components
    }
}

```
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

typedef long long ll;

struct Comp {
    vector<int> cities;
    int deg1;
    unordered_map<int, int> sec; // other component -> count
};

vector<int> parent;
vector<Comp> comp;
vector<vector<int>> city_comps; // for each city, list of components (size 0-2)
ll ans = 0;

int find(int x) {
    while (parent[x] != x) {
        parent[x] = parent[parent[x]];
        x = parent[x];
    }
    return x;
}

void linkCityToComponent(int city, int comp_id) {
    auto& clist = city_comps[city];
    auto& cdata = comp[comp_id];
    if (clist.empty()) {
        clist.push_back(comp_id);
        cdata.cities.push_back(city);
        cdata.deg1++;
    } else {
        // city already has exactly one component
        int other = clist[0];
        auto& otherComp = comp[other];
        // city was deg1 in other, now becomes sec
        otherComp.deg1--;
        otherComp.sec[comp_id]++;
        // update new component
        cdata.cities.push_back(city);
        cdata.sec[other]++;
        // update city
        clist.push_back(comp_id);
    }
}

void unionComp(int ru, int rv) {
    // ensure ru's city list not larger than rv's
    if (comp[ru].cities.size() > comp[rv].cities.size()) {
        swap(ru, rv);
    }
    Comp& small = comp[ru];
    Comp& large = comp[rv];

    // compute delta
    ll delta = 0;
    int overlap = 0;
    if (small.sec.count(rv)) overlap = small.sec[rv];
    int large_excl_total = large.cities.size() - overlap;

    for (int city : small.cities) {
        bool has_rv = false;
        int D = -1;
        for (int cid : city_comps[city]) {
            if (cid == rv) {
                has_rv = true;
            } else if (cid != ru) {
                D = cid;
            }
        }
        if (has_rv) continue;
        int already = 0;
        if (D != -1) {
            auto it = large.sec.find(D);
            if (it != large.sec.end()) already = it->second;
        }
        delta += large_excl_total - already;
    }

    ans += delta;

    // merge small into large
    for (int city : small.cities) {
        bool has_rv = false;
        int X = -1;
        for (int cid : city_comps[city]) {
            if (cid == rv) {
                has_rv = true;
            } else if (cid != ru) {
                X = cid;
            }
        }
        if (has_rv) {
            // overlapping case: city has both ru and rv
            // update large: remove from sec[ru], add to deg1
            auto it = large.sec.find(ru);
            if (it != large.sec.end()) {
                if (--(it->second) == 0) large.sec.erase(it);
            }
            large.deg1++;
            // remove ru from city_comps
            auto& clist = city_comps[city];
            for (auto it2 = clist.begin(); it2 != clist.end(); ++it2) {
                if (*it2 == ru) {
                    clist.erase(it2);
                    break;
                }
            }
        } else {
            if (X == -1) {
                // city only has ru
                large.cities.push_back(city);
                large.deg1++;
                // update city_comps: replace ru with rv
                auto& clist = city_comps[city];
                for (auto it2 = clist.begin(); it2 != clist.end(); ++it2) {
                    if (*it2 == ru) {
                        clist.erase(it2);
                        break;
                    }
                }
                clist.push_back(rv);
            } else {
                // city has ru and X
                large.cities.push_back(city);
                large.sec[X]++;  // city now contributes as sec X in large

                // update component X
                Comp& compX = comp[X];
                auto it = compX.sec.find(ru);
                if (it != compX.sec.end()) {
                    if (--(it->second) == 0) compX.sec.erase(it);
                }
                compX.sec[rv]++;

                // update city_comps: replace ru with rv
                auto& clist = city_comps[city];
                for (auto it2 = clist.begin(); it2 != clist.end(); ++it2) {
                    if (*it2 == ru) {
                        *it2 = rv;
                        break;
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }

    // DSU link
    parent[ru] = rv;
    // clear small data to free memory (optional)
    small.cities.clear();
    small.sec.clear();
    small.deg1 = 0;
}

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(0);

    int n, m;
    cin >> n >> m;

    // initialize structures
    vector<int> home_id(n+1, -1);
    vector<int> dest_id(n+1, -1);
    city_comps.assign(n+1, vector<int>());

    // We'll dynamically expand parent and comp as we create nodes.
    parent.reserve(2*n + 5);
    comp.reserve(2*n + 5);

    auto createNode = [&](int city) -> int {
        int idx = parent.size();
        parent.push_back(idx);
        comp.emplace_back(); // default construct
        linkCityToComponent(city, idx);
        return idx;
    };

    ans = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < m; ++i) {
        int a, b;
        cin >> a >> b;

        if (home_id[a] == -1) {
            home_id[a] = createNode(a);
        }
        if (dest_id[b] == -1) {
            dest_id[b] = createNode(b);
        }

        int u = home_id[a];
        int v = dest_id[b];
        int ru = find(u);
        int rv = find(v);
        if (ru != rv) {
            unionComp(ru, rv);
        }
        cout << ans << "\n";
    }

    return 0;
}
```

world_final_2025-H.content:
Ever since you arrived at your university, you have been a tireless advocate for introducing the brand-new martial-arts-plus-card-based sport of Contact Bridge to the school (and the world). Finally, after a great deal of (really persistent and annoying) advocacy on your part, you have obtained permission and funding from your dean to build a grand new arena for the sport! Well, technically it is not so much an "arena" as a "broom closet," and maybe not "grand" so much as "cramped," and the "new" is also debatable. But the sport of the future has to start somewhere!

Unfortunately, you just realized that you are going to need a score display in order to run the games. In Contact Bridge, the score for a team starts at 0 and, after various repeatable actions, may be incremented by certain fixed amounts. There is also a maximum value – if the team's score would be incremented above the maximum, it will instead be capped there. You want the team's score to be visible at all times, so you will need to prepare some signs, each with a single digit printed on it, that can be arranged to show the score.

Unfortunately the dean's "funding" is running short, and these signs are expensive. Figure out the minimum set of signs you need to purchase to show any score that is possible to achieve during the game. Note that you won't need any 9 signs, as any 6 sign can be turned upside-down to make a 9.

**Input**

The first line of input contains two integers $m$ and $n$, where $m$ $(1 \leq m \leq 10^{18})$ is the maximum score value, and $n$ $(1 \leq n \leq 10)$ is the number of different ways of scoring. This is followed by $n$ lines, each containing an integer $p$ $(1 \leq p \leq 1000)$, which is the number of points awarded for a type of action in the game. No two types of action are awarded the same number of points.

**Output**

For each digit from 0 to 8 in increasing order, output two integers: the digit and the number of signs with that digit that you need to purchase. Omit digits where the number of signs needed is 0.

**Examples**

Input#1
```
1000 4
60
100
222
650
```
Output#1
```
0 3
1 1
2 3
3 1
4 3
5 1
6 3
7 2
8 3
```

Input#2
```
967 1
1000
```
Output#2
```
0 1
6 2
7 1
```

Please write a cpp code to solve the above problem. You need to read the input from stdin and output the result to stdout.

world_final_2025-H.reasoning_content:
We need to solve a problem about the minimum set of digit signs needed to display any score that can be reached during a game. The game has initial score 0, and there are n types of increments, each of amount p (positive integers). The process: start at 0, each action increments the current score by one of the p values. However, there is a maximum score m: if an increment would go above m, the score is capped at m (i.e., we cannot exceed m). So the set of reachable scores is the set of numbers that can be obtained by repeatedly adding increments (with repetition allowed) but capped at m. Essentially, it's the set of numbers that are representable as sums of the p_i (with any nonnegative integer numbers of each) but where the sum is less than or equal to m; but note that when we exceed m, we cap at m; so m itself is reachable if we can exceed or reach exactly? The description: "if the team's score would be incremented above the maximum, it will instead be capped there." So if the current score is s and we add p, the new score is min(s+p, m). Starting from 0. So we can eventually reach m via a sequence that would exceed m, ending at m. Also we might reach scores up to m. But is every number <= m reachable? Not necessarily, depending on the increments. We need to consider the set of all numbers that can appear as scores during any possible sequence of actions, considering the capping. Since we start at 0, and we can apply increments in any order, repeating. After each action, the score is some value in [0,m]. The set of reachable scores is the set of numbers that can be obtained as min(sum of p_i's, m) for some multiset of increments (order doesn't matter except maybe capping? Actually capping applies after each increment; so if we have an increment that would exceed m, the score becomes m; subsequent increments keep it at m (since min(m+p, m) = m). So once you reach m, you stay at m. So the reachable scores are all numbers that can be expressed as x, where x = min(t, m) for some t that is a nonnegative integer linear combination of p_i. And it includes m if there exists some combination sum >= m (since min(>=m, m)=m). Actually also m is reachable if there exists some t such that t >= m; but we may also exactly hit m. So the set S = { min(t, m) : t ∈ T } where T = { sum_i a_i * p_i | a_i ∈ Z_{\ge 0} }.

Thus S = { x ∈ [0, m] : exists t ∈ T with t = x (if x < m) or t >= m (if x = m) }.

But note: For x < m, we require an exact representation: x ∈ T. For m, we require that T contains some number >= m, i.e., that the additive semigroup generated by p_i is not contained entirely below m (i.e., its Frobenius coin problem). Actually if the p_i have gcd > 1, they might skip numbers, but here we just care about the set of reachable scores.

We'll implement DP as function:

int max_digit(int g, long long low, long long high, int target_digit, bool combined) {
    string hi = to_string(high);
    int len = hi.size();
    string lo = to_string(low);
    lo = string(len - lo.size(), '0') + lo;
    vector<vector<vector<vector<int>>>> memo(len, 
        vector<vector<vector<int>>>(g, vector<vector<int>>(2, vector<int>(2, -2))));
    function<int(int,int,bool,bool)> dfs = [&](int pos, int rem, bool tl, bool th) -> int {
        if (pos == len) {
            return (rem == 0) ? 0 : -1; // -1 indicates invalid
        }
        int &res = memo[pos][rem][tl][th];
        if (res != -2) return res;
        int lo_d = lo[pos] - '0';
        int hi_d = hi[pos] - '0';
        int st = tl ? lo_d : 0;
        int en = th ? hi_d : 9;
        int best = -1;
        for (int d = st; d <= en; ++d) {
            int nrem = (rem * 10 + d) % g;
            int ntl = tl && (d == lo_d);
            int nth = th && (d == hi_d);
            int inc = 0;
            if (combined) {
                if (d == 6 || d == 9) inc = 1;
            } else {
                if (d == target_digit) inc = 1;
            }
            int cur = dfs(pos+1, nrem, ntl, nth);
            if (cur >= 0) {
                best = max(best, cur + inc);
            }
        }
        return res = best;
    };
    int ans = dfs(0, 0, true, true);
    if (ans < 0) ans = 0; // just in case, but should not happen.
    return ans;
}

int max_digit_count(long long low, long long high, int g, int target_digit, bool combined) {
    string hi = to_string(high);
    int len = hi.size();
    string lo = to_string(low);
    lo = string(len - lo.size(), '0') + lo;

    vector<vector<vector<vector<vector<int>>>>> memo(len, 
        vector<vector<vector<vector<int>>>>(g, 
            vector<vector<vector<int>>>(2, 
                vector<vector<int>>(2, 
                    vector<int>(2, -2)))));

    function<int(int,int,bool,bool,bool)> dfs = [&](int pos, int rem, bool started, bool tl, bool th) -> int {
        if (pos == len) {
            if (!started) {
                // number is 0
                if (rem == 0) {
                    if (!combined && target_digit == 0) return 1;
                    else return 0;
                } else return -1;
            } else {
                return (rem == 0) ? 0 : -1;
            }
        }
        int &res = memo[pos][rem][started][tl][th];
        if (res != -2) return res;
        int lo_d = lo[pos] - '0';
        int hi_d = hi[pos] - '0';
        int st = tl ? lo_d : 0;
        int en = th ? hi_d : 9;
        int best = -1;
        for (int d = st; d <= en; ++d) {
            bool nstarted = started || (d != 0);
            int nrem = (rem * 10 + d) % g;
            bool ntl = tl && (d == lo_d);
            bool nth = th && (d == hi_d);
            int inc = 0;
            if (nstarted) {
                if (combined) {
                    if (d == 6 || d == 9) inc = 1;
                } else {
                    if (d == target_digit) inc = 1;
                }
            } // else inc remains 0
            int cur = dfs(pos+1, nrem, nstarted, ntl, nth);
            if (cur >= 0) {
                best = max(best, cur + inc);
            }
        }
        return res = best;
    };
    int ans = dfs(0, 0, false, true, true);
    if (ans < 0) ans = 0; // fallback
    return ans;
}

Now BFS function for m ≤ LIMIT:

void solve_small(long long m, const vector<int>& p) {
    int M = (int)m;
    vector<char> visited(M+1, 0);
    queue<int> q;
    visited[0] = 1;
    q.push(0);
    while (!q.empty()) {
        int s = q.front(); q.pop();
        for (int inc : p) {
            int ns = s + inc;
            if (ns > m) ns = m;
            if (!visited[ns]) {
                visited[ns] = 1;
                q.push(ns);
            }
        }
    }
    int max_digit[9] = {0}; // 0..8
    int max_comb = 0;
    for (int s = 0; s <= M; ++s) {
        if (visited[s]) {
            string str = to_string(s);
            vector<int> cnt(10,0);
            for (char c : str) cnt[c-'0']++;
            for (int d=0; d<=8; d++) {
                if (cnt[d] > max_digit[d]) max_digit[d] = cnt[d];
            }
            int comb = cnt[6] + cnt[9];
            if (comb > max_comb) max_comb = comb;
        }
    }
    // output
    for (int d=0; d<=8; d++) {
        if (max_digit[d] > 0) {
            cout << d << " " << max_digit[d] << "\n";
        }
    }

1. Read m, n.
2. Read vector<int> p(n).
3. Compute g = accumulate(p.begin(), p.end(), p[0], gcd<int,int>).

   // preprocess
   vector<int> p_prime; for (int val : p) p_prime.push_back(val / g);
   int a0 = *min_element(p_prime.begin(), p_prime.end());
   vector<long long> dist(a0, LLONG_MAX);
   dist[0] = 0;
   priority_queue<pair<long long, int>, vector<pair<long long, int>>, greater<>> pq;
   pq.push({0,0});
   while (!pq.empty()) {
        auto [d, u] = pq.top(); pq.pop();
        if (d != dist[u]) continue;
        for (int pp : p_prime) {
            int v = (u + pp) % a0;
            long long nd = d + pp;
            if (nd < dist[v]) {
                dist[v] = nd;
                pq.push({nd, v});
            }
        }
   }
   long long Cprime = 0;
   for (int i=0; i<a0; i++) Cprime = max(Cprime, dist[i]);
   long long Th = g * Cprime; // may be up to ~1e6? Actually could be up to 1e9? But we argued <=1e6.
   // generate low range
   vector<int> low_max(9, 0);
   int low_comb = 0;
   if (Th > 0) {
        long long L = Th - 1;
        vector<char> reachable(L+1, 0);
        reachable[0] = 1;
        for (int coin : p) {
            if (coin > L) continue; // optional
            for (long long v = 0; v + coin <= L; ++v) {
                if (reachable[v]) reachable[v+coin] = 1;
            }
        }
        for (long long v = 0; v <= L; ++v) {
            if (reachable[v]) {
                string s = to_string(v);
                int cnt[10] = {0};
                for (char c : s) cnt[c-'0']++;
                for (int d=0; d<=8; d++) if (cnt[d] > low_max[d]) low_max[d] = cnt[d];
                int comb = cnt[6] + cnt[9];
                if (comb > low_comb) low_comb = comb;
            }
        }
   }
   // high DP
   vector<int> high_max(9, 0);
   int high_comb = 0;
   for (int d=0; d<=8; d++) {
        high_max[d] = max_digit_count(Th, m, g, d, false);
   }
   high_comb = max_digit_count(Th, m, g, 0, true); // combined
   // m counts
   string mstr = to_string(m);
   int mcnt[10] = {0};
   for (char c : mstr) mcnt[c-'0']++;
   int mcomb = mcnt[6] + mcnt[9];
   // final

        int ans;
        if (d != 6) {
            ans = max({low_max[d], high_max[d], mcnt[d]});
        } else {
            ans = max({low_comb, high_comb, mcomb});
        }
        if (ans > 0) {
            cout << d << " " << ans << "\n";
        }
   }

Define function:

int max_digit_count(long long low, long long high, int g, int target_digit, bool combined) {
    string hi = to_string(high);
    int len = hi.size();
    string lo = to_string(low);
    if (lo.size() < len) lo = string(len - lo.size(), '0') + lo;
    // memo: [pos][rem][started][tight_low][tight_high] -> int
    vector<vector<vector<vector<vector<int>>>>> memo(len,
        vector<vector<vector<vector<int>>>>(g,
            vector<vector<vector<int>>>(2,
                vector<vector<int>>(2,
                    vector<int>(2, -2)))));
    function<int(int,int,bool,bool,bool)> dfs = [&](int pos, int rem, bool started, bool tl, bool th) -> int {
        if (pos == len) {
            if (!started) {
                // number is 0
                if (rem == 0) {
                    if (!combined && target_digit == 0) return 1;
                    else return 0;
                } else return -1;
            } else {
                return (rem == 0) ? 0 : -1;
            }
        }
        int &res = memo[pos][rem][started][tl][th];
        if (res != -2) return res;
        int lo_d = lo[pos] - '0';
        int hi_d = hi[pos] - '0';
        int st = tl ? lo_d : 0;
        int en = th ? hi_d : 9;
        int best = -1;
        for (int d = st; d <= en; ++d) {
            bool nstarted = started || (d != 0);
            int nrem = (rem * 10 + d) % g;
            bool ntl = tl && (d == lo_d);
            bool nth = th && (d == hi_d);
            int inc = 0;
            if (nstarted) {
                if (combined) {
                    if (d == 6 || d == 9) inc = 1;
                } else {
                    if (d == target_digit) inc = 1;
                }
            }
            int cur = dfs(pos+1, nrem, nstarted, ntl, nth);
            if (cur >= 0) {
                if (cur + inc > best) best = cur + inc;
            }
        }
        return res = best;
    };
    int ans = dfs(0, 0, false, true, true);
    if (ans < 0) ans = 0;
    return ans;
}

int max_digit_count(long long low, long long high, int g, int target_digit, bool combined) {
    string hi = to_string(high);
    int len = hi.size();
    string lo = to_string(low);
    if ((int)lo.size() < len) lo = string(len - lo.size(), '0') + lo;
    int state_cnt = len * g * 2 * 2 * 2;
    vector<int> memo(state_cnt, -2); // -2 uncomputed, -1 invalid, else count.
    function<int(int,int,bool,bool,bool)> dfs = [&](int pos, int rem, bool started, bool tl, bool th) -> int {
        if (pos == len) {
            if (!started) {
                if (rem == 0) {
                    if (!combined && target_digit == 0) return 1;
                    else return 0;
                } else return -1;
            } else {
                return (rem == 0) ? 0 : -1;
            }
        }
        int idx = (((pos * g + rem) * 2 + started) * 2 + tl) * 2 + th;
        if (memo[idx] != -2) return memo[idx];
        int lo_d = lo[pos] - '0';
        int hi_d = hi[pos] - '0';
        int st = tl ? lo_d : 0;
        int en = th ? hi_d : 9;
        int best = -1;
        for (int d = st; d <= en; ++d) {
            bool nstarted = started || (d != 0);
            int nrem = (rem * 10 + d) % g;
            bool ntl = tl && (d == lo_d);
            bool nth = th && (d == hi_d);
            int inc = 0;
            if (nstarted) {
                if (combined) {
                    if (d == 6 || d == 9) inc = 1;
                } else {
                    if (d == target_digit) inc = 1;
                }
            }
            int cur = dfs(pos+1, nrem, nstarted, ntl, nth);
            if (cur >= 0) {
                best = max(best, cur + inc);
            }
        }
        return memo[idx] = best;
    };
    int ans = dfs(0, 0, false, true, true);
    if (ans < 0) ans = 0;
    return ans;
}

world_final_2025-D.content:
The International Center for Planetary Cartography (ICPC) uses rovers to explore the surfaces of other planets. As we all know, other planets are flat surfaces which can be perfectly and evenly discretized into a rectangular grid structure. Each cell in this grid is either flat and can be explored by the rover, or rocky and cannot.

Today marks the launch of their brand-new Hornet rover. The rover is set to explore the planet using a simple algorithm. Internally, the rover maintains a direction ordering, a permutation of the directions north, east, south, and west. When the rover makes a move, it goes through its direction ordering, chooses the first direction that does not move it off the face of the planet or onto an impassable rock, and makes one step in that direction.

Between two consecutive moves, the rover may be hit by a cosmic ray, replacing its direction ordering with a different one. ICPC scientists have a log of the rover's moves, but it is difficult to determine by hand if and when the rover's direction ordering changed. Given the moves that the rover has made, what is the smallest number of times that it could have been hit by cosmic rays?

**Input**

The first line of input contains two integers r and c, where r (1 \leq r \leq 200) is the number of rows on the planet, and c (1 \leq c \leq 200) is the number of columns. The rows run north to south, while the columns run west to east.

The next r lines each contain c characters, representing the layout of the planet. Each character is either '#', a rocky space; '.', a flat space; or 'S', a flat space that marks the starting position of the rover. There is exactly one 'S' in the grid.

The following line contains a string s, where each character of s is 'N', 'E', 'S', or 'W', representing the sequence of the moves performed by the rover. The string s contains between 1 and 10000 characters, inclusive. All of the moves lead to flat spaces.

**Output**

Output the minimum number of times the rover's direction ordering could have changed to be consistent with the moves it made.

**Examples**

Input#1
```
5 3
#..
...
...
...
.S.
NNEN
```
Output#1
```
1
```

Input#2
```
3 5
.###.
....#
.S...
NEESNS
```
Output#2
```
0
```

Input#3
```
3 3
...
...
S#.
NEESNNWWSENESS
```
Output#3
```
4
```

**Note**

Explanation of Sample 1: The rover's direction ordering could be as follows. In the first move, it either prefers to go north, or it prefers to go south and then north. Note that in the latter case, it cannot move south as it would fall from the face of the planet. In the second move, it must prefer to go north. In the third move, it must prefer to go east. In the fourth move, it can either prefer to go north, or east and then north. It is therefore possible that it was hit by exactly one cosmic ray between the second and third move, changing its direction ordering from N??? to EN?? where '?' stands for any remaining direction.

Explanation of Sample 2: It is possible the rover began with the direction ordering NESW, which is consistent with all moves it makes.

Please write a cpp code to solve the above problem. You need to read the input from stdin and output the result to stdout.

bool isAcyclic() {
    int indeg[4] = {0};
    // compute indegree from adjacency (bool adj[4][4])
    for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++)
        for (int j = 0; j < 4; j++)
            if (adj[i][j]) indeg[j]++;
    // topological sort
    queue<int> q;
    for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++)
        if (indeg[i] == 0) q.push(i);
    int cnt = 0;
    while (!q.empty()) {
        int u = q.front(); q.pop();
        cnt++;
        for (int v = 0; v < 4; v++) {
            if (adj[u][v]) {
                indeg[v]--;
                if (indeg[v] == 0) q.push(v);
            }
        }
    }
    return cnt == 4;
}

   Initialize counts[4][4]=0, adj[4][4]=false.

        while (right+1 < n) {
            int nxt = right+1;

                int a = dir[nxt];

                    int a = dir[nxt];

        maxR[left] = right;
        // Now remove step left (if left <= right)
        if (left <= right) {
            for (int v : edges[left]) {
                int a = dir[left];
                int b = v;
                cnt[a][b]--;
                if (cnt[a][b] == 0) {
                    adj[a][b] = false;
                }
            }
        }
   }

bool adj[4][4] = {false}.

Helper function bool acyclic():

    int indeg[4] = {0};
    for (int u = 0; u < 4; u++) {
        for (int v = 0; v < 4; v++) {
            if (adj[u][v]) indeg[v]++;
        }
    }
    queue<int> q;
    for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++) if (indeg[i] == 0) q.push(i);
    int visited = 0;
    while (!q.empty()) {
        int u = q.front(); q.pop();
        visited++;
        for (int v = 0; v < 4; v++) {
            if (adj[u][v]) {
                indeg[v]--;
                if (indeg[v] == 0) q.push(v);
            }
        }
    }
    return visited == 4;
}

            if (cnt[a][b] == 0) {

            }

}

I'll write the code in a structured manner.

Pseudo:

int main() {
    ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(0);

    int r,c;
    cin >> r >> c;
    vector<string> grid(r);
    int sx, sy;
    for (int i=0; i<r; i++) {
        cin >> grid[i];
        for (int j=0; j<c; j++) {
            if (grid[i][j] == 'S') {
                sx = i; sy = j;
            }
        }
    }
    string moves;
    cin >> moves;
    int n = moves.size();
    vector<int> dir(n);
    // map char to int
    for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
        char ch = moves[i];
        if (ch == 'N') dir[i] = 0;
        else if (ch == 'E') dir[i] = 1;
        else if (ch == 'S') dir[i] = 2;
        else if (ch == 'W') dir[i] = 3;
    }

    // simulate positions
    vector<int> px(n+1), py(n+1);
    px[0] = sx; py[0] = sy;
    const int dr[4] = {-1, 0, 1, 0};
    const int dc[4] = {0, 1, 0, -1};
    for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
        int d = dir[i];
        px[i+1] = px[i] + dr[d];
        py[i+1] = py[i] + dc[d];
    }

    // precompute other_valid for each step
    vector<vector<int>> other_valid(n);
    for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
        int x = px[i], y = py[i];
        int d = dir[i];
        bool valid[4] = {false};
        // check each direction
        for (int k=0; k<4; k++) {
            int nx = x + dr[k];
            int ny = y + dc[k];
            if (nx >= 0 && nx < r && ny >= 0 && ny < c && grid[nx][ny] != '#') {
                valid[k] = true;
            }
        }
        // ensure that d is valid (should be)
        for (int k=0; k<4; k++) {
            if (k != d && valid[k]) {
                other_valid[i].push_back(k);
            }
        }
    }

    // two-pointer to compute maxR
    vector<int> maxR(n);
    int cnt[4][4] = {0};
    bool adj[4][4] = {false};

    auto acyclic = [&]() -> bool {
        int indeg[4] = {0};
        for (int u=0; u<4; u++) {
            for (int v=0; v<4; v++) {
                if (adj[u][v]) {
                    indeg[v]++;
                }
            }
        }
        queue<int> q;
        for (int i=0; i<4; i++) {
            if (indeg[i] == 0) q.push(i);
        }
        int visited = 0;
        while (!q.empty()) {
            int u = q.front(); q.pop();
            visited++;
            for (int v=0; v<4; v++) {
                if (adj[u][v]) {
                    indeg[v]--;
                    if (indeg[v] == 0) {
                        q.push(v);
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return (visited == 4);
    };

    int right = -1;
    for (int left = 0; left < n; left++) {
        // extend
        while (right + 1 < n) {
            int nxt = right + 1;
            int a = dir[nxt];
            // add edges
            for (int b : other_valid[nxt]) {
                cnt[a][b]++;
                if (cnt[a][b] == 1) {
                    adj[a][b] = true;
                }
            }
            if (acyclic()) {
                right = nxt;
            } else {
                // revert
                for (int b : other_valid[nxt]) {
                    cnt[a][b]--;
                    if (cnt[a][b] == 0) {
                        adj[a][b] = false;
                    }
                }
                break;
            }
        }
        maxR[left] = right;
        // remove step left
        if (left <= right) {
            int a = dir[left];
            for (int b : other_valid[left]) {
                cnt[a][b]--;
                if (cnt[a][b] == 0) {
                    adj[a][b] = false;
                }
            }
        }
    }

    // compute minimal segments using jump game
    int jumps = 0;
    int cur_end = 0;
    int farthest = 0;
    for (int i=0; i<n; i++) {
        farthest = max(farthest, maxR[i] + 1);
        if (i == cur_end) {
            jumps++;
            cur_end = farthest;
            if (cur_end >= n) break;
        }
    }
    int ans = jumps - 1;
    cout << ans << endl;

    return 0;
}

Now, double-check indexing: In the jump algorithm, we used maxR[i]+1 which is the next start after covering up to maxR[i]. For i = n-1, maxR[i] should be n-1 (since at least itself), so maxR[i]+1 = n. So farthest may become n. Then if cur_end becomes n, we break. All good.

But what about when i reaches n-1 and we haven't covered all? Actually if cur_end < n, we need to ensure we eventually jump to n. The algorithm's loop will go to i up to n-1; but if cur_end is less than n after the loop, it means we haven't reached the end. But that would happen only if some moves cannot be covered by any segment? But each step individually is feasible, so maxR[i] >= i => maxR[i]+1 >= i+1, so farthest will eventually reach n.
